stance_by_source.summary: Weekly chart, no commentary: price has collapsed to ~0.088 and is resting on a weekly demand zone, with huge overhead supply (0.20, 0.32) and the old 0.55+ extension target still drawn far above.
Generic 'buy the pullback to the breaker, target the supply above' note — but the attached chart is actually SUIUSDT, not DOGE, so it carries no real DOGE levels.

Thin context-only mention with no levels.
Buy 0.2028-0.205 for 0.26-0.27 then 0.55-0.58, invalid below ~0.195 — that stop has long since been blown.
Buy 0.185-0.189 for 0.20/0.215 then macro 0.55, invalid below 0.16 — stop already broken.
Buy ~0.18 for 0.28 then 0.58, but explicitly 'structure stays bearish until 0.2865 is reclaimed' — that reclaim never happened.

conflicts: Price has crashed to about 0.088, which is below EVERY buy level and EVERY stop-loss Zero ever published for DOGE (0.18, 0.20, 0.12, 0.14; stops at 0.16, 0.195, 0.12). All of those tactical long setups are invalidated and must not be acted on.

Direction is contested: market structure is clearly broken/bearish (a chain of lower lows down to 0.088, every stop hit), while Zero's long-term thesis is still bullish accumulation toward 0.55 — these point opposite ways over different horizons.
The mention dated 2026-05-11 captioned 'DOGE' is actually a SUIUSDT chart, so it gives no usable DOGE levels despite being one of the more recent items.
The only fresh DOGE data (2026-06-16 weekly) is a chart with no written entry/target/stop, so the actionable levels here are read off the image, not stated by Zero in text.
plain_english: Dogecoin has fallen hard to roughly 0.088, which is below every single price Zero ever told you to buy at and below every price he told you to bail at — so all of his older trade ideas are off the table, not opportunities. The chart trend is firmly down, yet Zero's long-term plan has always been to slowly buy dips and hold for around 0.55, so the long-term and short-term pictures disagree. The price is currently sitting on a weekly support shelf, but with no fresh confirmation and a wall of resistance overhead, this is not a confident buy. Do not chase it: wait for the weekly support around 0.088 to clearly hold (or only scale in tiny accumulation amounts), and treat a weekly close under roughly 0.07 as the sign to stop.
